title: Test suite validates plumbing, not conversation fidelity — no end-to-end multi-turn coherence test
what_failed: The keystone coherence bug (T-148) shipped with 92 green tests because every test exercises a component in isolation with synthetic data. test_compression_fallback.py used content='msg {i}' plain strings — the exact shape that hid the dict-block bug, because production stores assistant turns as {'type':'text','text':...} dicts. There is no test that drives a real multi-turn conversation through process_input and asserts the model actually receives prior turns (both sides).
where_failed: Whole suite — gap, not a line. verify.py reports PASS while T-141/T-142/T-143 (core conversation bugs) sit open.
why_likely: Tests were written bug-by-bug, each mocking the narrow unit it covers. No one wrote the test that asserts the emergent property ('a conversation stays coherent') because it's harder and fuzzier than unit tests. Green suite created false confidence — the exact failure mode called out in the project audit.
migration_plan: 1. Build testing/test_conversation_golden.py: a fake LLM client (records the messages + system prompt it receives) wired into PiAgent so no API is hit.
2. Drive a scripted 8-turn conversation through process_input in EACH mode (root, normie).

3. Assert invariants on what the fake client received: (a) Pi's own prior replies appear in context, (b) a fact stated at turn 1 is still reachable at turn 8, (c) no orphaned tool_use/tool_result pairs, (d) messages never empty.
4. Mark non-costly (fake client, no network) so verify.py runs it every time.
5. Extend the fake-client harness so future context/memory tickets get a real regression bed instead of synthetic-string unit tests.
implementation_notes: test_context_fidelity.py (added in T-148) is the unit-level version of this. T-152 is the integration-level version: the full process_input path, all modes, scripted conversation. This is the test whose absence let the keystone bug exist.
analysis: PERSPECTIVE: This is the highest-leverage non-bug ticket in the batch. Your green suite is currently a liability — it tells you the product works when it doesn't. Until there's one test that asserts 'a conversation stays coherent end to end,' every coherence fix is unprotected and will regress. Build the fake-client harness once; it pays for itself on every memory/context ticket after. I'd do this immediately after T-149.
